The argument is invalid because when **E and G are true and F is false**, the premises hold (since \( E \supseteq F \) and \( G \supseteq 
eg F \) are both true), but the conclusion \( 
eg G \lor 
eg E \) (which simplifies to \( 
eg (G \land E) \)) is false. This violates the requirement for validity. No other option provides a valid counterexample.
